text: If fate is a milestone, then we are the grist. There is nothing we can do. So I wish for strength. If I cannot protect them from the wheel, then give me a strong blade, and enough strength... to shatter fate.

Are you any different!? You sacrificed yourself to save me that time!! At that time were you thinking about complicated stuff like 'this is my Shinigami duty'!? That's not what sacrificing yourself is!! At the very least... I'm different!! I haven't accepted any commitment... If things get bad I might run away... since I'm not a good enough person to be able to sacrifice my life for total strangers. But... unfortunately I'm also not trash that can live happily without paying back his debts!
context: To Rukia Kuchiki regarding the meaning of sacrifice.

text: Big brothers... you know why they're born first? To protect the little ones that come after them!! What kind of brother says that he'll KILL his own sister?! Even a MONSTER shouldn't say that!!
context: To Acidwire about the responsibility of a brother.

text: At first, I thought 'I want to protect Mom.' The ones to protect increased with the birth of my sisters. I kept going to a dojo for the sake of protecting them. I got a bit stronger. The ones I thought I wanted to protect became more and more.
context: Ichigo reflecting on his motivation for training.

text: I'm not superman, so I can't say anything big like I'll protect everyone on earth. I'm not a modest guy who will say it's enough if I can protect as many people as my two hands can handle either. I want to protect a mountain-load of people.

All right. Let's do this Chad. You keep doing your thing and don't fight for yourself, but fight for me. And I will fight for you. If you put your life on the line to protect something, then I'll put my life on the line to protect it too.
It's meaningless to just live, it's meaningless to just fight. I want to win!
‘Our pride’, you said? So that pride you're saying to be so precious, is that connected to killing Rukia? Is it? Then I should stomp on it as you said. 'Cause that's why I obtained this Bankai!
Damn that Kenpachi! He really went all out chasing me down. That was scary as hell. His reiatsu was terrifying. To think that I actually defeated someone like that. I don't even believe it myself.
context: Ichigo reflecting on his battle with Kenpachi.

text: The difference... in strength... what about it? Do you think I should give up... just because you're stronger than me...? I've always known you were strong. Nothing I see now will change my mind. I will defeat you Ulquiorra.
No... I said I'm not gonna do it. Is this... Is this any way to win?
context: To Ulquiorra Cifer, refusing to use his hollow form to win.

text: A way of dealing with you? I haven't had a way of dealing with anyone so far. I just defeated them because I had to defeat them. That's all there is to it. You may be a different level than the other Espada, but I don't give a shit. It's the same. If I have to beat you, then I'll beat you. That's all there is to it.

If you didn't tell me until now, you must have had your reasons. And that's your problem, not mine. I wouldn't know how to start. I can't think of any way of asking that wouldn't mean traipsing all over your feelings and staining them with dirt. So I'll wait. Until you decide someday that you want to tell me, until you decide it's time for me to know. Until that time comes, you don't have to say a thing.

I'm not saying I don't remember your blade. I'm saying I don't remember your heart. When you cross blades, you can tell a little of what your opponent's thinking. I'm not saying you can read their mind or anything like that, but you can tell what kind of resolve lies behind their blade, whether they respect you or look down on you. That kind of thing, you can tell. When I'm actually fighting, there's no time to think about it, so I don't usually realize until afterwards, but in general, the stronger the opponent is, the more of that 'heart' seems to come across.
Look Muramasa, or whatever your name is... You missed one important thing. You've been calling me a Shinigami this whole time. I'm a Human!

That's right... I never felt a sense of superiority because I could see spirits. And I never once thought that I could make a living or help someone with it. I just longed for a life where I couldn't see them. And I finally got what I always wanted.
context: To himself about his former life without powers.

text: Every week, when it is time, they stick to the TV. They watch my actions in the comfort of their families after dinner. Children watch my actions and their hearts leap. They see me stand up to evil spirits and... they know what courage is...! Do you understand, boy? With them watching, I can't run away from the enemy...!
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki, explaining the responsibility of his status as a TV hero.

text: I have abilities... I can understand the difference between myself and the enemy. However, boy... your ideas made an impression on me! The way of fighting where one thinks people's safety first indiscriminately... your way of fighting is befitting of a hero! Allow me to call you battle buddy.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki, declaring him a battle buddy.

text: Sorry to keep all of my viewers waiting. Your Don Kanonji, my Don Kanonji, everyone's Don Kanonji has returned! IIII haaaave reeeturrrned!!!
context: Don Kanonji's dramatic return during the Aizen battle.

text: ...Run away? Do you speak those words to a hero such as myself? What an uninformed girl. I shall enlighten you. A hero who runs away from battle will not be called a hero by the children.
context: To Tatsuki Arisawa, explaining his duty as a hero.

text: Even the most cool headed Shinigami have a person whom they could never cut. That is inevitable. By rooting out that, I've swept aside Shinigami up till now.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki, explaining his twisted philosophy regarding how he hunts Shinigami.

text: A beast with developed vision is always controlled by that vision.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki during their battle, mocking Ichigo's focused aggression.

text: Byakuya-sama, please find my sister. Then after you find her, please do not tell her that I am her sister. Without telling her anything, please Byakuya-sama, please protect my sister no matter what. I abandoned her, so I don't deserve to be her sister, so I hope that she can be Byakuya-sama's sister.
context: Hisana's final request to Byakuya on her deathbed regarding Rukia.

text: Even at the end, I am still asking more of you. I am very sorry I couldn't return Byakuya-sama's love. I am sorry. Being with Byakuya-sama for the past five years was like a dream come true for me, Byakuya-sama.
context: Hisana expressing her gratitude and regrets to Byakuya before passing away.

text: What are you doing, partner? If you just stand there... I'll kill you!
context: To Ichigo during their inner world confrontation.

text: He's strong... Zangetsu... train him well... because one day, that power will become all mine.
Who am I? I have no name!
Who am I? How can you ask such a stupid question? I am who I am!
arc: // (Filler: The New Captain Shusuke Amagai Arc)

text: I'm going to keep getting closer and closer to you. And not just day after day, oh no. It's going to happen much faster than that! See I'm already closer to you than I was a moment ago. And I'll keep getting closer and closer to you, until I swallow you up and then, you'll disappear.
That "Zangetsu" thing you're talking about... aren't you holding it right there? Or maybe... could it possibly be the one... that I have here?
context: Mocking Ichigo about who truly wields the blade.

text: I am... Zangetsu!

Ichigo, I don't know if you understand or not,...but, Zangetsu and I have always been one and the same. Both Zangetsu and I are a part of your spiritual power, and I was apart of Zangetsu! We all share the same body, and whenever the 'dominant' person changes, the outer appearance also changes.
context: Explaining the nature of their power.

text: Ichigo, what's the difference between a king and his horse? ...There's only one answer. Instinct! ...That's why you're weaker than me, Ichigo!
context: The King and the Horse speech.

text: I'm not gonna have it, Ichigo. I don't know about Zangetsu, but I refuse to carry a king who's weaker than me and get cut to ribbons with him. If you're weaker than me, then I'll destroy you, and take your crown for myself.
Shit. Looks like your instincts, and your desire to fight, aren't completely gone. Guess there's no way around it. You got me. I have to recognize you as the king, for now.
What am I? I'm his true form. I guess you could say I'm his instincts.
I'm gonna devour Ichigo and take over his body for good. That's my instinct.
Well, there's no point if my landlord's gonna croak.

Right after I was born, Soul Society ordered the mod souls to be destroyed... and the day after I was born I was already chosen to die! Everyday while I was in that pill I was afraid. As I watched my friends disappear each day. Even after I got lucky and escaped the storage among other pills, I feared being discovered and killed. As I trembled in fear I would think... life isn't something that should be taken from others. I was born like this! I should at least have the right to live and die freely! Whether it's humans or insects... or even us... it's the same. That's why I won't... kill anything.
context: Kon explaining his origins and philosophy about life.

text: Nice angle!
context: Reaction after Rukia steps on him.

text: Extra large...puffy wuffy...booby woobies!
Well, I could tell whether or not Orihime is the real one...if she would invite me into her Valley of the Gods!
How could you make this poor kid cry? What a worthless brother!
context: Criticizing Ichigo for making Yuzu cry.

text: You're all crazy, aren't you? Who is Rukia Kuchiki to you people? Isn't she just a friend? Is it really necessary to go this far just for a friend?
context: To the Ryoka (Ichigo Kurosaki), questioning their motivation to rescue Rukia.

text: I was proud of my hair color that my brother admired. I was happy to be told such things, so I grew my hair out ever since I was little. Soon after I entered middle school, 3rd years said they didn't like my hair color and cut it. I had no choice but to cut the rest short to even it out. I couldn't tell brother. 'I felt like changing it' is what I said to dodge the question. Then my brother died soon after. I became alone. I stopped talking, even at school, and became more alone than ever before. The one who stopped me from being that way was Tatsuki-chan. Tatsuki-chan yelled at me for backing down and she looked out for me. I could grow my hair out again thanks to her. I will never have short hair again, because my long hair is proof of my faith in Tatsuki-chan.
context: Orihime explaining the sentimental significance of her long hair to Tatsuki.

text: I will not forgive those who hurt Tatsuki!
context: Furious reaction while defending Tatsuki Arisawa from Numb Chandelier.

text: Thank you, Tatsuki. Please do not worry, Tatsuki. Because Tatsuki will come find me, that's why I can go anywhere without worry. If the place I'm going is somewhere Tatsuki can't go, then please wait for me here, I will come back for sure. Come back to Tatsuki's side.
context: Internal thought regarding her bond with Tatsuki.

text: It's not frustrating at all. It's just... it's going to be lonely, not being able to fight alongside everyone. But being lonely isn't nearly as bad as being in the way. If I'm just going to be a hindrance to Kurosaki-kun, to everyone, then it's far better to be lonely.
context: Reflecting on her feelings of being a hindrance to the group.

text: Kurosaki-kun, you know I had a lot of things I wanted to do... I want to be a teacher... I also want to be an astronaut... and also make my own cake shop... I want to go to the sweets bakery and say 'I want one of everything'... Ohhhh, I wish I could live life five times over. Then I’d be born in five different places, and I’d stuff myself with different food from around the world... I’d live five different lives with five different occupations... and then, for those five times... I’d fall in love with the same person...
context: Confessing her desires while Ichigo is asleep.

text: Everything that has occurred... it reverses it all, and destroys the very event itself... I will return the Hōgyoku to before it ever existed!
context: Orihime realizing the true nature of her power regarding the Hogyoku.

text: Kurosaki-kun... doesn't assure us he is going to win for that reason. Kurosaki-kun is a kind person. Whenever he uses strong words, it's like he's making a promise. I believe that, he makes a promise to himself. I think that he expresses his feelings in words so that he will follow through. So that's why it's okay. When Kurosaki-kun says he is going to win, he'll win for sure.
context: Explaining her faith in Ichigo to Nelliel.

text: Maybe it's impossible to feel exactly the same way as another person, but it's possible for people to care about one another and to place their hearts as close together as they can manage. I'm sure that's what it means to make your hearts as one.
context: Trying to understand the connection between hearts while speaking to Ulquiorra.

text: Do not die! Kurosaki-kun! You don't have to win... You don't even have to do your best... Just don't get hurt anymore...
context: Desperate plea to Ichigo during his fight.

text: Don't make me laugh! It is your fault that Rukia lost her power. That's why her sentence is so heavy. Don't you get it? It's all because of you that Rukia is going to die! What right do you have to say you want to save her? Quit joking around.
I am really a tramp down to my bones. I hate myself. I bark at the moon, but I don't have the courage to jump and grab it.
context: Reflecting on his past and his feelings of inadequacy.

text: I never won once against Captain Kuchiki. Ever since Rukia left, I trained every day, but I still failed. He is too strong. Fighting to get Rukia back is nothing but an impossible dream to me. Kurosaki, I'm probably shameless for doing this, but I have to ask you. Please, you have to save Rukia!
context: Renji swallowing his pride to ask Ichigo for help.

text: I have always been afraid, always been pretending to follow you closely, always been pretending to sharpen my teeth, when the truth is, I am scared to death just treading on your shadow.
context: Confessing his deep-seated fear of Byakuya Kuchiki.

text: For a long time now, before I joined the Gotei 13, the only person that I have always wanted to surpass is you, Captain Kuchiki!
context: Declaring his ultimate goal to surpass Byakuya.

text: Showing concern for a warrior's life on the battlefield is an insult to that warrior.
context: A stern remark to Ichigo during their battle.

text: What the hell are you on? I didn't come here to chit-chat with you. I came here to beat the hell out of you guys. I don't give a shit about your life story. If you wanna chat, you can do it all you want while I kill you. But I'm not gonna go easy on you.
context: To Szayel Aporro Granz before their fight.

text: A man who raises his hands on a woman is trash. If I have to become trash in order to survive... It'll be just like dying.
context: To Jackie Tristan during their confrontation.

text: Even if you save that kid here, nothing will matter if you don't become a Shinigami! Saving him because he's right in front of you!? Don't be naive. A Shinigami has to treat all spirits equally! You cannot just conveniently save those you can see, those you can reach. Don't save that kid with such half-heartedness. If you want to save him now... accept that you must save all spirits. To go anywhere for them... to even give your life to save them, make that kind of commitment!
context: Lecturing Ichigo about the resolve required to be a Shinigami.

text: ...If I ask, will you answer? ...It's your problem. A deep, deep problem. I have no right to know. I don't have a method of stepping into the depths of your heart without getting it dirty. So I'll wait. When you want to talk, when you think it's okay to talk... Talk to me. Until that time, I'll wait.
context: Expressing her patience and trust in Ichigo.

text: Whether I like him, whether I hate him, it's just all so troublesome. Love, companionship and friendship... it's nothing but trouble.

When the time comes when I have to leave, I have to cut myself off from them all. Love, companionship and friendship. It's troublesome. It's really, really nothing but a saddening emotion. All Shinigami shouldn't have these unnecessary feelings. What kind of disgraceful behavior is this... Rukia Kuchiki?
context: Reflecting on her duty as a Shinigami.

text: I...I was the one who dragged him into this!! It's my fault he died!! Since it's my fault he died...! Is it so wrong to go to him?
Don't even try to move one step from there. If you try to follow me... I'll never forgive you!
context: Warning Ichigo not to follow her.

text: We must not shed tears. That is the defeat of the body by the heart. Because, for us, this thing called 'the heart' can become nothing less than proof that one's existence is superfluous.

I'm not scared. I'm content with my life. Meeting Renji and the others, adopted by Nii-sama, Kaien-dono's guidance, then Ichigo's attempted rescue. No pain, no sadness, no regrets. There is nothing for my heart to linger in this world. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. And goodbye.
context: Coming to terms with her fate before execution.

text: In a battle, the ones who get in the way are not the ones that lack power, but the ones that lack resolve.

I... I did take your life Kaien. I think that sin is something that will never vanish, no matter how many years pass. If killing me will lift your spirits even a little bit... then I will gladly offer you my life. However, I came to Hueco Mundo to save Inoue... my friend. Until I rescue Inoue Orihime I cannot offer you my life no matter what you may say...!
context: Confronting the entity disguised as Kaien.

text: At that time I was consumed by a single insecurity. From the start, I had little talent with a sword. My Kidō grades were good when I was in the Shinō Academy, but even that was average among the Gotei divisions. Was it really alright for me to be here? Where does my heart lie? Why am I here in the Gotei Divisions?
context: Reflecting on her early days in the Academy while confronting Aaroniero.

text: I remembered where the heart lies. What's inside you is indeed Kaien's spirit body, but... Kaien is not there... Kaien... left his heart with me!
I know the loneliness of being a prisoner. I know the joy you feel when your friends come to rescue you and the fear of them being injured and defeated.
You are not a Hollow nor an Arrancar. You're a human. The duty of a Shinigami is to protect Humans such as yourself. I have no intention of harming you.
Those are cool-looking broom, Lotte.
The fact that you only have two escorts is a bit anticlimactic, but having those annoying walls fixed in place suits me just fine, since rushing a conversation isn't really in my nature.
context: Greeting Orihime upon her arrival in Las Noches.

text: Do not ask any questions. Do not say anything. You have no rights. What you hold in your hand is the rope to the guillotine above your friends' necks. Nothing more. Understand, girl. This is not a negotiation, it's an order.

What would you have me say? 'Don't worry, I'm sure he's still alive'? Ridiculous. I am not here to comfort you. I don't understand why are you so fixated on life and death? At any rate, your friends will soon be annihilated. So what if that's one step closer? They should have seen this coming from the start. If they couldn't see it coming, the fault lies in their own foolishness. Laughing them off as a group of idiots would be sufficient. Why can't you do that? If it were me, and my friends entered Hueco Mundo without first gauging their strength, I'd be infuriated by their stupidity.
context: Taunting Orihime regarding the fate of her friends.

text: Even if you rise up a thousand times, there will be no victory for any of you.
context: During the final battle with Ichigo.

text: Hearts, you say? You Humans are always so quick to speak of such things. As though you carry your hearts in the very palms of your hands. But this eye of mine perceives all. There is nothing that it overlooks. If this eye cannot see a thing, then it does not exist. That is the assumption under which I have always fought. What is this 'heart'? If I tear open that chest of yours, will I see it there? If I smash open that skull of yours, will I see it there?
context: Philosophical debate on the concept of the 'heart'.

text: Do not lose your composure, do not break your stance, stretch out your awareness, and do not let your guard down for even a second.
context: Combat advice during the invasion sequence.

text: I see. This. Yes. This thing in my hand is the heart?
context: Final realization of the 'heart' upon his death.

text: If you do not know it, then I shall enlighten you. This is the form of true despair.

No matter how similar your appearance and techniques may be to those of an Arrancar their power is as far apart as heaven and earth. When a Shinigami or Human wishes to gain power, imitating Hollows is an appropriate course of action. However, because they are imitating, Humans will never be equal to Hollows.
context: Comparing Ichigo's Hollow form to a true Arrancar.

text: You are a fool, Ichigo Kurosaki. You challenge an opponent whose strength terrifies you and think that you can win. It is beyond my comprehension. If it is due to what you Humans call a 'heart', it is because of that heart that you suffer injuries and it is because of that heart that you will lose your life.
Kill me. Quickly. I no longer have the strength to walk. If you do not cut me down now, then this fight will remain forever unsettled.
...I see. No mercy? How very Hollow like. I don't mind, I have lost to you. I no longer have any meaning. DO IT.
You want an attack full of "killing intent"? Then come get me. My bow is full of it.
Sensei, I want to get stronger. I'll become a very strong Quincy and then protect everyone from Hollows. Then father will definitely agree with the way of the Quincy.
context: Flashback discussing his motivation with his grandfather.

text: I swear on the pride of the Quincy, I will kill you.
Apologize to everyone and never appear in front of me again, then I will spare your life. If you don't, I will shoot you again with three times as much power as the last shot.

She knows that if she tries to help, she'll just be in the way. Plus, even if she is able to help Kurosaki wouldn't want her to. She knows all of this, but she is still clenching her whole body, as if trying to stop it from taking action. Inoue is waiting, praying that you will be safe, believing you will return. Don't lose Kurosaki. If you lose, I will never forgive you!
context: Internal thoughts regarding Orihime and Ichigo during the battle against Byakuya.

text: You're asking if this is a bow? Of course it is. You think I would use anything else?
context: Demonstrating his Quincy powers to Ichigo.

text: I have not exterminated you so that you can relay a message to Aizen. Tell him the Quincy are here and that in reality it's not the Shinigami he should fear but rather the Quincy!
context: Delivering a message through Aisslinger Wernarr.

text: The right to choose life or death lies with the victor and the victor of this battle is me. From her perspective, my leaving her alive here might seem like an insult but I have no intention of doing things their way.
I am composed, that's why I've got some leeway when fighting against you.
The more complex a person, the weaker they are.
Ichigo wants to save her. Ichigo went all out for her, and that's enough. That's all the reason I need to put my life on the line for anything.
context: Discussing the motivation to save Rukia.

text: That's right. Very important. Maybe even more important than my life.
context: Speaking about the coin his Abuelo gave him.

text: Sorry. The truth is, I don't like doing things like dealing the deathblow to opponents who have lost the will to fight. But this time, it seems I can't even say such naive things.
This power of mine seems to be less like a Shinigami or a Quincy and more like you Hollows.
Because you fought me with all your might, I was able to grasp my power. Thanks. Because of you, I'll be able to tough it out ahead. I'll spare your life and pray for your good fortune.
Whether you can see them or not, as long as you don't believe, it's the same as them not existing.
context: Discussing the existence of ghosts/spirits with Yuzu and Ichigo.

text: From now on, it's my turn to protect my brother.
context: Reflecting on her resolve to care for Ichigo after the battle with Aizen.

text: ...Listen, all right? I don't know what's been going on with you... But you should quit trying to keep me in the dark. I'm not one of your school buddies, you know! I'm an adult! I don't need a brat like you worrying about me or trying to protect me from whatever you've got yourself mixed up in! So if there's something wrong, then come right out and say it! Kids like you... are meant to be able to rely on us adults!
context: Ikumi confronts Ichigo at the Unagiya Shop, telling him to stop hiding his troubles.

text: I don't need your apologies! If you feel bad about it, try actually coming in to work! Doesn't matter to me, you know! You can come 'cause you're lonely... 'Cause you're having a hard time... Or even just 'cause you happened to be in the area... Ever since the day I hired you, I figure I'm basically like a big sister to you... so you can come see me whenever you like! I told you before, didn't I?! Helping out kids like you is what us grown-ups are here for!
context: Ikumi offers her support to Ichigo, emphasizing her role as a 'big sister' figure to him.

text: All throughout history, be it royals or samurai or whatever, this world has always been ruled by minorities! You're not the ones in the wrong. It's this world that's become too lenient towards idiots. It's time we changed all that. This time, we're gonna be the ones at the top of the food chain.
context: Ginjō explaining his worldview and philosophy to Riruka and Xcution.

text: Time will try to constantly try to catch up with you. And with a roar, it will overcome you. Do not look ahead. Hope only lies within the unforeseeable current that will close in from behind.
context: Poetic monologue representing Ginjō's perspective on time and destiny.

text: I fully intend to crush the Shinigami for betraying me as they did. I thought you would try to convince me that was wrong. And yet, you don't even bother? Why? Those eyes... They're the eyes of a man who's trying to understand me. Of a man putting himself in my shoes, treating me as an equal. Are you trying to say you already understand why I'm doing this, and choose to deny me regardless?
context: Ginjō speaking to Ichigo, questioning Ichigo's resolve and understanding of his motivations.

text: Ichigo... If our positions had been reversed... Would things have still turned out the same way? Would you have ended up like me? Tell me, Ichigo... If our positions were reversed... Could we have been...
context: Ginjō's final words to Ichigo during his defeat.

text: As your underling, I can't just turn my back on this situation! I appreciate your concern, Tsukishima-san, but I value your honor over my own life!
context: Expressing his undying loyalty to Tsukishima during the Fullbringer arc.

text: I'm gonna show you how man I am by pounding your face into the ground!!
context: Confronting Ikkaku Madarame during their battle.

text: I decided a long time ago to use my life for Tsukishima-san's sake!!
context: Stating his resolve to fight for Tsukishima against Ikkaku.

text: Tsukishima-san is crazy strong! I'd die for him without a second thought!!
context: Proclaiming his admiration and willingness to sacrifice for Tsukishima.

text: You're the greatest, and that's all there is to it! So don't you go dyin' on me, Tsukishima-san! You're the most awesome guy there is! So you can't go dyin', got that?! You're s'posed to be invincible!
context: Begging Tsukishima not to die after his defeat.

text: What're you thanking me for?! Saving you ain't nothin' I oughta be thanked for! I'm just doin' my duty! I wouldn't be much of an underling otherwise, would I?

Why did you have to die? Why would you do that?! Why?!! Why would you do that, Ginjō?!! Ginjō, you taught me how to use my power. You taught me how to fight. So why, Ginjō. Why?! Why did you never teach me what I should do when you were gone?!
context: Tsukishima mourns Ginjō after witnessing his defeat and death at the end of the Fullbring arc.

text: I've never formed a real connection with anyone, Fullbring or no Fullbring.
context: Yukio reflects on his isolated life while speaking to Jackie Tristan after the events of the battle.

text: Over the next three years, I'm going to grow up a little and I'll expand my business to three times its current size. Then I'll come back. I'll come and find you again so you can all work for me. I'm sure none of you misfits would be able to live a decent life without the money I can provide, after all.
context: Yukio shares his future plans with Jackie regarding his intent to expand his business and eventually reconnect with the surviving Xcution members.

text: It's true, I always did hate my ability. But strangely enough, now I've lost it I find myself envying Giriko, who managed to hold on to his until the moment he died. At some point I must have realized that these Fullbrings we bear are all that really holds us together. That must be why I'm feeling this way.
context: Jackie reflects on the loss of her power and the bond shared by the members of Xcution while speaking to Yukio.

text: How unfortunate! You found her? Pardon me. It wasn't my intention to traumatize you. Before you could have noticed, I should have chopped her into unrecognizable pieces.
context: Aizen taunting Hitsugaya after he discovers the wounded Hinamori.

text: You should learn this well, Hitsugaya-kun. Admiration is the emotion furthest from understanding.
context: Aizen explaining the nature of Hitsugaya's attachment to Hinamori.

text: The Sousuke Aizen you knew...never existed to begin with.
context: Aizen speaking to Renji, shattering his perception of Aizen's past persona.

text: From the very start, no one has stood upon the heavens. Not you, not I, nor even gods. However, that intolerable vacuum has come to an end. Henceforth... I shall stand upon the heavens.
context: Aizen's declaration of his ascension to Jūshirō Ukitake.

text: You should not dwell on the betrayal that you can see. The truly terrifying betrayal is that which lies utterly hidden from your eyes, Captain Hirako.
context: Aizen lecturing Shinji Hirako on the nature of betrayal.

text: I never even dreamed that the power of the ten Espada I went to so much trouble to gather could possibly be inferior to that of myself alone.
context: Aizen's cold assessment of his Espada's strength compared to his own.

text: Did you think that a display of force would be enough to overwhelm me? How foolish. In fact, I would say that the very concept of 'power' itself is different for me than for you. Allow me to demonstrate. This is the true meaning of 'power'.
context: Aizen speaking to Sajin Komamura during their confrontation.

text: I will not take your lives. With the measure of power that you possess, you are unlikely even to lose consciousness from those wounds. Stay there, helpless and defeated, and observe carefully as this battle reaches its conclusion.
context: Aizen speaking to the Captains after defeating them.

text: Your Ryūjin Jakka is indeed the most powerful Zanpakutō in existence. There is no doubt about that. Were I to fight against it directly, it would likely overpower even me. However, if one abandons all other abilities and focuses his strength entirely on a single specialized skill, then he can overcome even the greatest of powers.
context: Aizen explaining to Yamamoto why Wonderweiss was created specifically to seal Ryūjin Jakka.

text: I suppose it is only to be expected. You have my pity. There is no such thing as 'truth' or 'lies' in this world; there never has been. There is only plain, hard facts...
context: Aizen explaining his philosophical view on 'truth' to Ichigo.

text: The Hōgyoku is able to manifest the desires of those around it. However, this manifestation will not be successful unless the possessors of those desires have the strength to carry it out.
context: Aizen explaining the function of the Hōgyoku to Isshin Kurosaki.

text: The laws of nature are for beings who must live their lives bound by them. Now, shall we carry on? To a place beyond all laws.
context: Aizen speaking to Gin Ichimaru regarding his transcendence.

text: So if you are killed, the gate to Muken will be closed for all eternity. Did they expect I would go so far as to take your life in order to steal the key to this place? I never did like those old fools in Central 46...
context: Aizen speaking to Shunsui Kyōraku while being released from Muken.

text: Yhwach, the world that you desired to create may indeed have been devoid of fear. However, in a world without the fear of death, men could not face that fear and seek out hope... We call it 'courage'.
context: Aizen's reflection on courage and death during his final confrontation with Yhwach.

text: The betrayal you can see is trivial. What is truly frightening and much more lethal, is the betrayal that you don't see.
context: Aizen speaking to Shinji Hirako, revealing the depth of his deception.

text: You shouldn't be using such strong remarks, it only makes you look weak.
context: Aizen speaking to Hitsugaya Toshiro at the Central 46 chamber.

text: A victor must speak not of the current state of the world, but of how it should rightfully be.
context: Aizen lecturing Jūshirō Ukitake about the nature of victory and leadership.

text: Ichigo Kurosaki... All of the battles you've fought up to now... were simply a part of my plan.
context: Aizen revealing to Ichigo that his journey was orchestrated by him.

text: All creatures want to believe in something bigger than themselves. They cannot live without blind obedience. And to escape the pressure of that trust, those in whom faith is placed in turn look for someone higher than themselves. And then those people in turn look for someone even stronger. That is how all Kings are born. That is how all Gods are born.
context: Aizen's speech to the Arrancar regarding the nature of faith and power.

text: Since the beginning, no one has ever stood in the heavens. Neither you, nor me, not even God himself. But that unbearable vacancy in the throne of Heaven shall be filled. From this day forth, I will stand in heaven.

It's time. I'll let you choose. Would you rather I kill you, or would you like to jump into that pit and die? Please look this way and show me your expression. I want to see just how ugly you can get. Doesn't matter which one you choose, you'll still die either way.
This is inevitable, I guess. Ugly people are always jealous of pretty people. It's only natural.
If you were a little bit less ugly, I might have spared your life.
Be sure not to make the same mistake again when you reincarnate. Ugly things are completely worthless. Since you're already ugly, I'll give you a clean, pretty death to compensate.
I haven't lost yet! I'm sorry, but you have to be dead for it to be considered a loss in the Eleventh Division.
It seems that you don't understand the 11th Division at all. We're all gonna die eventually, so we fight before we do! That's what we're all about!
context: Defining the philosophy of the 11th Division to Hisagi.

text: Just look at that; that overjoyed look on his face. Ikkaku is having so much fun. After such a long time, he's able to fight a strong opponent. 'We must help each other at all costs'--it's not like that. You see, with us, it's 'We must not help each other at all costs'.
context: To Keigo Asano regarding Ikkaku's fighting style.

text: I refuse to look upon something so hideous.
Rival, huh? You talk like you've acknowledged my strength, but I'm not interested in flattery from someone with such a messed-up hairstyle as that.

I'm a member of the Eleventh Division... It's full of idiots obsessed with putting their lives on the line. Thanks to the style of our Division, there's always been a kind of unwritten rule that all our Zanpakutō have to be direct-combat types... I don't want any of my fellows in the Eleventh Division to see this Zanpakutō's ability.
context: Explaining why he hides his true Shikai ability.

text: Captain Aizen's words... his voice... Captain's everything calms my restless spirit. I'm so glad I came... I'm so lucky to serve under you, Captain Aizen.
context: Internal monologue reflecting on her deep admiration for Aizen.

text: Please save Captain Aizen. What Captain Aizen is doing is bad, but I bet he has a good reason for it. Yeah, that's it... Surely Captain Ichimaru or someone is forcing him...
context: Pleading with Tōshirō Hitsugaya regarding Aizen's supposed betrayal.

text: Exactly what it sounds like. The shape this thing takes just kind of irritates me. I mean, just look at it. Wouldn't you say this shape is made for reaping lives?
context: To Findorr Calius regarding the appearance of his Zanpakutō, Kazeshini.

text: He who fears not the blade he wields has no right to wield that blade.
context: To Findorr Calius during their battle.

text: Have you learned to fear yet? It looks like we're finally evenly-matched.
context: To Findorr Calius after releasing his Zanpakutō.

text: Every time I draw my blade, every time I face the enemy - on the inside, I'm always taking a half-step back!
context: To Kaname Tōsen during their confrontation.

text: As I thought, you are no longer Captain Tōsen. Your old, sightless self would have evaded an attack of this level with ease.
context: To Kaname Tōsen as he pierces his throat.

text: I've always looked up to you, and you have my utmost respect. However, that doesn't give you the right to speak ill of Captain Tōsen!
context: To Kensei Muguruma regarding his mentor.

text: There we go again, old men and their stupid arguments. What a pain.
context: Reacting to the bickering of the Captains.

text: If you make Hinamori shed even one drop of blood... I'll kill you.
context: Threatening Gin Ichimaru regarding Momo Hinamori.

text: Hinamori! How many times do I have to tell you? It's not "Hitsugaya-kun". It's "Captain Hitsugaya".
context: Correcting Momo Hinamori on his title.

text: You're being too lenient with an opponent you've only hit once. Haven't you ever heard of following through?

I can't say I expected your speed and range to increase so dramatically the moment you released. It's a good thing I played it safe. This little trick is a one-time deal, so I didn't really want to have to use it quite so early. Don't underestimate it. The power of a Shinigami.
Since you were kind enough to lecture me, allow me to tell you something. You are in the greatest danger when you execute a flawless strategy. That, too, is the way of battle.
Wielding one's blade out of duty alone is what it means to be a captain. Wielding one's blade out of hatred is nothing more than petty violence. That is not what we would consider battle.
context: Defining the philosophy of a Captain.

text: You've got it exactly right, Aizen. I wield this blade out of hatred. I didn't come here to do battle. I'm here to slice you to pieces as a sheer act of violence!
context: Rejecting his Captain's duty in favor of personal vengeance.

text: As long as I can strike you down, I would gladly abandon my status as captain right here and now!
context: Determination to defeat his enemy regardless of status.

text: After the battle with Aizen, I put everything I had into mastering my Bankai. I honed it until it could be called a 'true Bankai'. But now, that Bankai has been taken from me. I must cast aside that which I have lost. I don't have time to be fruitlessly hoping for its return. I must keep moving forward. Moving forward...
context: Reflecting on the loss of his Bankai and his resolve to keep fighting.

text: What idiotic rules are you talking about? You misunderstood. Even when the guardian loses, the guardian may still not open the door. If the guardian lost, then that would mean... he must be executed.
context: To Jidanbō Ikkanzaka regarding the rules of the Seireitei gates.

text: I was just taking a walk and I dropped by to tease you.
context: To Rukia Kuchiki while she is imprisoned.

text: Would you like me to save you?... Just kidding.
It would have been nice if my capture lasted a little longer. Farewell, Rangiku. Sorry.
context: To Rangiku Matsumoto during his final moments.

text: I'm not too fond of sad stories.
It's nice to see he's still got spirit in him.
context: To Kaname Tōsen regarding Izuru Kira.

text: I thought you were an interesting kinda kid, but I guess you're actually pretty creepy.

No point doin' anything now. Me an' Aizen go way back, but even I've never seen him like this. Well, I guess that's only natural, seein' as he's gone an' absorbed the Hōgyoku. You get it, right? There's no point fightin' any more. They're all gonna get killed, an' so are you. The end.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki during the battle against Aizen.

text: Feelings, eh? Nah, 'fraid not. Don't got none o' them. Didn't I tell ya, back when we first met? I'm a snake. Cold of flesh and devoid of heart. My tongue flicks back and forth, ever in search of new prey, and if I like what I find, I swallow 'em whole.
I've decided. I'm gonna become a Shinigami. I'll become a Shinigami, and change things. I'll make it so you never have to cry again.
context: As a child, speaking to Rangiku Matsumoto.

text: If you were to turn into a snake tomorrow and begin devouring humans, and from the same mouth you started devouring humans, you cried out to me 'I love you,' would I still be able to say 'I love you' the same way I do today?
context: In his dying thoughts regarding Rangiku Matsumoto.

text: The Royal Guard's duty is to protect the Soul King Palace, and the Gotei 13 is to protect Soul Society. As someone who bears the name of Gotei, don't go crying to us about how help didn't come just because you couldn't do anything. It's already bad enough you're a disgrace to that title!
context: To Suì-Fēng and the other Gotei 13 captains upon the Royal Guard's arrival at the Soul Society following the first Quincy invasion.

text: Worry not. I have no doubt. Naught but my debt to Genryūsai-dono is what inspires my all. Everyone shunned me due to my appearance. He found me, when I was one gone astray. The debt which I owe that gentleman shall I answer with all my spirit. I do not hesitate. Should that gentleman call it justice, then even my death shall be justice!
context: To Tetsuzaemon Iba regarding his loyalty to Captain-Commander Yamamoto.

text: ...With our hands, we will open Tōsen's eyes!
context: To Shūhei Hisagi about their resolve regarding Kaname Tōsen.

text: You're right. I have not yet introduced myself. I am Seventh Division Captain Sajin Komamura. And though it shames me, I am just as you say...a mere worm of a man.
context: To Choe Neng Poww during their confrontation.

text: I never thought the day would come when I would be forced to protect someone from your blade.
context: To Kaname Tōsen upon finding him attacking his comrades.

text: Ichigo Kurosaki did not seek that Hollow transformation of his own volition. But you are different. You chose that forbidden path for yourself, despite already possessing more than sufficient power as a Shinigami! You have debased yourself, Tōsen!
context: To Tōsen, condemning his choice to use Hollow powers.

text: It is your betrayal of your allies, betrayal of your friends, betrayal of your own subordinates - all for the purpose of obtaining this unnatural power that I refer to as debasement!
context: To Tōsen, highlighting the moral failing of his betrayal.

text: If our core beliefs are truly incompatible, then attempting to solve this with words is meaningless. For the sake of Soul Society, I must strike you down. I do not wish to do so. Having heard your true beliefs, I am now satisfied. In my heart, I have already forgiven you.
context: To Tōsen, expressing his final resolve.

text: That is why I swore to myself that I would become a true friend to you. If you bore great sadness, I would take it upon myself. If I obtained great joy, I would share it with you. If you strayed from your path, I would scold you. If you made terrible mistakes, I would forgive you. If the world scorned you, I would be your shelter. Somehow, I would give this man who had lost his love for the world the power to love it once more.
context: Internal monologue reflecting on his friendship with Tōsen.

text: When I was unable to bear the burden of living in shadow...When I abandoned my own family and fled, not caring for the shame I brought upon myself...It was Genryūsai-dono who took me in. That man, to whom I owe the greatest debt, has had his own life cut short at the hands of these Quincy. I have a duty...to avenge this humiliation!!
context: To his great-grandfather, explaining his reason for seeking revenge for Yamamoto.

text: I have not thrown my life away. I have merely gambled it. Genryūsai-dono gambled his own life on this battle. I see no reason why I should not do the same. From the moment I set foot on this battlefield, I had already left my life behind!
context: To Bambietta Basterbine during their battle.

text: What am I so happy about, you ask? I have found a new and unprecedented subject to observe! And what is more, it continually surpasses all of my expectation! What greater cause for joy can there be?!
context: To Ikkaku Madarame regarding Pernida Parnkgjas.

text: Information gained from literature is nothing more than that - information. I am not inclined to trust it without proving the facts for myself.
If you do not know, then do not act on your own judgement! You do not have my permission to die of your own accord! You will die only when I order you to do so!
context: To Nemu, scolding her for acting without orders.

text: To think the day would come when I would leave a battle to Nemu... I can scarcely stand the humiliation.
context: Reflecting on relying on Nemu in battle.

text: I cannot find any words to argue. To think that a half-baked scientist like yourself would help me to notice my own hubris. I must look quite the fool.
context: To an apparition of Szayelaporro Granz.

text: Where is the fun in controlling someone with no will of their own, might I ask?
context: To Giselle Gewelle, criticizing her ability.

text: Oh, do be quiet. I have no need for chatter from my test subjects. This is an experiment. You are Subject #1. The rights of refusal or acceptance are out of your hands!
My, my. The tool defying its own master. Did it really think it would get away with that? It was designed such that if it were ever to bite at me, then it would instantly self-destruct.
Ooh, a Quincy. They are a rare breed. I haven't seen a live one for many years. However, I'm sorry, but I've already finished studying your kind. I have absolutely no interest in you.
Silence! No matter what reason, to he who disrupts this household’s iron harmony, only a punishment of blood can be rendered!
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki, establishing the household dynamic.

text: Around the time we started dating, she said that my hand is cool when I'm smoking a cigarette. Now that I think about it, that was the only time your mom complimented me on my looks. So every year I smoke on this day only. In front of her.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki, reflecting on Masaki Kurosaki.

text: If I were to blame Masaki's death on you, Masaki would get mad at me. It's not anyone's fault that Masaki died. It's just that, the woman I fell in love with was a woman who could die protecting her son. And don't forget, you are the guy the woman that I loved gave her life to protect.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki regarding Masaki's death.

text: Live well, age well, and go bald well. And die after me. And... if you can, die smiling. If I can't, I won't be able to face Masaki. Don't hesitate to act. Sadness is a cool thing to shoulder, but you're still too young.
I'll take care of everything at the house! While I'm still around, I won't allow anyone to hurt my family. Ah! What I just said was so cool! That sentence is pretty useful.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki, projecting his character.

text: I'm sorry, Mr. Hollow, but Ichigo's not in right now. As a replacement or something, how about playing with me instead?
context: To Grand Fisher during their initial confrontation.

text: Even if you have no interest in me, I'm still here to kill you, so if you don't treat me as your opponent it would be a problem.

Let me teach you the basics. All captain class Shinigami control the size of their Zanpakutō, otherwise everyone would be swinging around swords the size of skyscrapers like yours. Remember that you can never judge the strength of an opponent by the size of their sword. You should have at least known that much before you challenged me, kid.
context: To Grand Fisher, demonstrating his true power.

text: You don't need to make it all so complicated! I've just gotta give up being a Shinigami and protect her for the rest of her life, right?! Of course I'll do it! If I let the girl who saved my life die because of some stupid regrets, I'd have to laugh in my own face!
context: To Kisuke Urahara regarding the decision to save Masaki.

text: Such excuses are of no value. Alone he fought and dealt with it, thus he was not permitted to lose. Fools who cannot understand that, even after this, shall not be of any usage. He is an eyesore. Get him out of my sight.
context: To his subordinates after defeating Renji Abarai.

text: Shall I tell you the difference between you and I? It's level. It's like the story of the monkey trying to capture the moon. No matter how he struggles, it's just the moon's reflection on the water he sees. So he only sinks into the water. Time after time, he sinks.
context: To Renji Abarai explaining the gap in their power.

text: With your ability, you will find it impossible to make me fall on even one knee.
context: To Renji Abarai during their battle.

text: Be proud, as someone who, after being struck by this power, is still able to retain the form of his body.
context: To Renji Abarai after using his Shikai.

text: Congratulations. Very admirable. That time, your fang definitely reached me.
context: To Renji Abarai, acknowledging his strength after the battle.

text: After adopting you, I swore in front of my parents' grave that it would be my last time in breaking the rules, and from that time on, no matter what happens, I would follow and defend the rules to the very end.
context: To Rukia Kuchiki, explaining his past conflict between law and family.

text: When you were sentenced to death, I was dumbfounded. The oath to my parents to follow and defend the rules, or the promise to Hisana to protect her sister. Which one should I keep? Ichigo Kurosaki, you have my gratitude. Rukia, I am sorry.
context: To Rukia Kuchiki, expressing his regret.

text: I have already told you. You and I, our levels are as distant as the earth from the heavens. Must I spell it out for you? I have discarded my left arm, discarded my left leg, and yet even so, I have not nearly lowered myself to your level.
context: To Zommari Rureaux during their battle.

text: At what point did I ever claim that I was cutting you down in my capacity as a Shinigami? To justify the actions I have taken, I need but one reason. You raised your blade in contempt of my pride.
context: To Zommari Rureaux, explaining his motivation.

text: Do not overestimate yourself, Ichigo Kurosaki. There is none among the captains of the Gotei 13 to whom the aid of one such as yourself could be considered significant.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki upon their first encounter.

text: I decline. Finishing off a weakened opponent is a job better suited for a barbarian such as yourself.

I have nothing but contempt for the way you do battle. Rather than face your opponent in fair combat, you prefer to toy with your enemy, stealing away the things they hold dear. You are a coward. A shameless coward who deserves to die. Now, face me like a man. Before you have even finished swinging down your blade, I will strike you down and toss your worthless body aside.

I had always believed that battle was an art to be perfected through meticulous training and preparation. But today, for the first time, I was able to comprehend the thrill of indulging one's more primal instincts. You have my thanks. This battle was an enjoyable one.
context: To Shūkurō Tsukishima after their fight.

text: True enough, I owe you a great debt. And you have my thanks. However, you are Ichigo Kurosaki's enemy. And thus, no matter the debt you are owed, I will strike you down without hesitation.

I fear I will not last much longer. As a Captain of the Gotei 13, to be unable to defeat the foul intruders who dirty the soil of Seireitei, who have brought about the deaths of many soldiers and brought sadness to those soldiers' subordinates and families... to lose my life in defeat at the hands of such wretches brings me great shame. You, meanwhile, are a human. By all rights, you should never have become involved in this conflict. Indeed, you should never even have come to this place. Yet now, at the end of my life, I ask you to forgive me the vile hubris of making one request of you. Please... protect Soul Society.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki while mortally wounded.

text: I have learned all too well that I am still far too weak to allow myself to grow headstrong. As I have been lucky enough to survive, I must strive to once more achieve strength enough to justify the pride I once held.
Fear is never born from nothing. It is born from the tiny insecurities that linger within our hearts.
If you wield your blade without regard for your own life, you will find yourself unable to protect anything.
It would appear that you are making some kind of mistake. Attempting to use me as a hostage will achieve nothing.
context: To Szayelaporro Granz, during their confrontation in Las Noches.

text: Ichigo, is it? That's a nice name. Men with the word Ichi in their names are often powerful and talented.
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki during their initial encounter.

text: I just can't understand. Even though there's a fair amount of distance between us, when two people are engaged in a fight, only a rookie would remove a hand from his sword.
What are you talking about? We're still fighting! One of us will die before it's over! I, Ikkaku Madarame, 3rd Seat of Zaraki's Division, will never walk away from a fight like a coward!
context: Refusing to give up the fight against Ichigo.

text: Damn... How could I not die after losing so much blood! What a shame to get saved by the enemy. Damn, if only I could move, I would kill you right now!
context: Reacting to Ichigo saving him after their duel.

text: I was never interested in Kidō in the first place. Unlike you, my mom didn't make me a do-it-all kind of guy at birth.
context: To Tetsuzaemon Iba regarding his combat preferences.

text: Idiot. I told you long ago when I taught you how to fight, didn't I? That I have no intention of telling anyone besides you and Yumichika, that I have Bankai. If it got out that I can use Bankai, there'd be others besides you telling me to become a captain. I have no intention of becoming a captain. Becoming a captain would mean that I can't fight under Captain Zaraki. If you wish to surpass Captain Byakuya Kuchiki, then I only have one wish. To fight and die under that man's command.
context: To Renji Abarai regarding his refusal of a captaincy.

text: You sure have a different way of getting down to business. 'To announce your name and rank' is a custom taught to us when we learn how to fight. It's known as the last courtesy. If a person dies in combat, at least you get to know the name of the one that brought death to you. Third Seat of Zaraki's Division, Ikkaku Madarame. As for the lowlifes who ignore this etiquette, I simply kill them after they know who did them in.
context: To Edrad Liones before their fight.

text: Cut the flattery. You can stop your admiration. It's only natural to show our true levels of Reiatsu. Whether it is worthy of praise is something you will learn after you die.
context: To Edrad Liones regarding their power levels.

text: You're too soft, Ichigo. You really think you're gonna win a fight like this without taking any lives? Finish them off properly! That's how you show respect to the enemy who started this battle.
context: Admonishing Ichigo for his hesitation in battle.

text: No man's life is worth more than another! If you're gonna put your life on the line, you do it as equals! I don't care if you're brothers, or master and apprentice, or whatever! You should only die for a guy who'd die for you! There's nothing honorable about throwing your life away as though it's nothing! That's just showing off like a kid!
context: To Moe Shishigawara, lecturing him on the value of life.

text: I had no name. I had no parents. I was from the 79th District of North Rukongai, Kusajishi. I had never seen anything in colors other than bloody red. But then you appeared, from the 80th District of North Rukongai, Zaraki. Even deeper in the darkness. You demolished the chaotic world that was dyed in blood, then you gave me a name. If it wasn't for you, I wouldn't be here today.
context: Reflecting on her past and the day she met Kenpachi Zaraki.

text: Since that day, you have been everything to me in this world.
context: Reflecting on her absolute devotion to Kenpachi Zaraki.

text: We'll get stronger, Ken-chan. Together, let's get stronger. I know that Ken-chan is the best. So let's get stronger! You and me together.
I don't know, I never really counted days until I met you.
context: To Gin Ichimaru, answering his question about her birthday.

text: That bad habit you had of always disappearing without telling me where you are going...still hasn't changed.
Oh, my. So you do listen after all. I was thinking your ears were as bad as your face and your style.
context: To Emilou Apacci during the battle in Fake Karakura Town.

text: You vanished without leaving a single thing for me to remember you by. That's what I always truly hated about you. And yet, if you had left me anything like that, I'm sure I would never have been able to take another step away from it. I suppose you must have seen right through me. Thank you, Gin. It's that about you...that I always truly loved.
context: Reflecting on Gin Ichimaru after his death.

text: I don't know, I never really counted days until I met you.
context: To Gin Ichimaru, answering his question about her birthday.

text: That bad habit you had of always disappearing without telling me where you are going...still hasn't changed.
Oh, my. So you do listen after all. I was thinking your ears were as bad as your face and your style.
context: To Emilou Apacci during the battle in Fake Karakura Town.

text: You vanished without leaving a single thing for me to remember you by. That's what I always truly hated about you. And yet, if you had left me anything like that, I'm sure I would never have been able to take another step away from it. I suppose you must have seen right through me. Thank you, Gin. It's that about you...that I always truly loved.
context: Reflecting on Gin Ichimaru after his death.

text: Don't get it? Can't blame ya. How you fight? How you attack? No, no, no. What's wrong? It's all wrong! You've used your Zanpakutō as tools? Treated them like subordinates? Relied on them like partners? As family? As friends? Role models? Students? Pets? Acquaintances? Sweethearts? Lovers? Don't make me sick!!! That's what these guys are tryin' to tell you. You're looking at things completely the wrong way. Which is superior, the Zanpakutō or the Shinigami? Let's think about that to start with, yeah?!!
context: To Ichigo Kurosaki and Renji Abarai, challenging their understanding of their Zanpakutō.

text: You be lookin' at the No. 1 Zanpakutō creator! Not Jū, not Kyū, not Hachi, not Nana, not Roku, not Go-mai! Not Shi or San-mai! The name's Nimaiya Ōetsu! And I ain't lettin' no trash get past me here. So shut up, buckle down, and give it your best shot. 'Cause you're about to taste a real Division Zero battle.
context: Introducing himself to Yhwach's Schutzstaffel before engaging them.

text: Hugs from men are made to be dodged.
context: To Gerard Valkyrie after dodging his attempt to grab him.

text: This blade, "Sayafushi", is one of my failures. Its blade is too sharp, and its edge too smooth. No matter how much you use it, it'll never chip or scratch, and not a single drop of blood will stick to it. Hell, this thing would put professional blade sharpeners out of a job. But the worst part is, I can't make a scabbard that'll hold it. Without a scabbard, it hardly qualifies as a proper sword, so I couldn't send it down to Seireitei. That's why I'm pretty happy you've come all the way up here to the Spirit Palace! Thanks to you, I got my chance to use this little fella. I really appreciate it, Y.H.!
context: Explaining his sword Sayafushi to Yhwach.

text: When you say you're lowering the lethal dose of my blood, that means if I have lots of blood in my body, I'll die, yeah? Then if I just let tons of that blood out 'til it's below the lethal dose, I'll be all good, right?
context: Finding a workaround to Askin Nakk Le Vaar's Deathdealing ability.

text: As a member of your school, I would only be able to imitate you. The role of a right-hand man should be to compensate for those areas which you cannot address!
context: To Genryūsai Shigekuni Yamamoto, explaining his philosophy as his lieutenant.

text: Because our captain is weak and sick, I'm usually in charge of everything! Therefore you may call me "Captain" Kaien if you like!
context: To Rukia Kuchiki, introducing his personality and role within the division.

text: Remember that as long as you're in my division, I will stand by you even if I die.
context: Reassuring Rukia Kuchiki of his loyalty and protection.

text: Every time you and I connect with each other, a little bit of heart is born between us. Heart isn't something inside you. But whenever you think, whenever you remember someone, that's when heart is born. If you were the only one alive in the whole world, heart wouldn't exist now, would it?
context: Explaining the concept of 'heart' to Rukia Kuchiki.

text: Whenever you fight from this point on, there is one thing that you must never do. And that is to die alone. Our bodies are composed entirely from Reishi. If we die, our bodies will disintegrate and become part of the reishi that makes up Soul Society. And when that time comes, where will your heart go? Your heart will be passed on to your friends. If you pass it on to your friends, then your heart will always live on within them. Kuchiki, that's why you must never, ever, ever die alone.
context: Teaching Rukia the true nature of their existence and the bond of the heart.

text: I dragged you into this. My stubbornness has caused you pain. I'm sorry. You must be torn apart inside. Thanks to you, my heart can still stay here.
context: Expressing regret and gratitude to Rukia during their final moments.

text: People discover their true, inner powers when they want to protect something. Their own lives, status, honor, loved ones, a principle...a fierce desire to protect is the key.
context: To Orihime Inoue and Yasutora Sado during their training session.

text: The heart and soul are closely linked. Your heart reveals what is most precious to you.
context: To Orihime Inoue and Yasutora Sado, explaining the connection between heart and power.

text: There are two types of battles, and as long as we place ourselves in the midst of battle, we must always continue to discern between them. The battle to protect life, and the battle... to protect pride...! Now... he is fighting for pride—his wife's pride, his subordinates' pride, and most of all, for his own pride. ...You may dismiss it as foolish stubbornness if you wish... But let him... let him continue to fight like this...
context: To Rukia Kuchiki, discussing the nature of battles.

text: To sink all the way to the level of allying with the Menos. What are you trying to do?
Friends are a good thing, even if they tread a different path.

I know that. But still, I'm afraid I must refuse! As far as I'm concerned, you are a child, and a girl, at that. I couldn't possibly fight someone like you! If you selfishly decide to fight me despite that, then I'll just make you go home myself! Now why don't you run along and play with a ball or something?
context: To Lilynette Gingerbuck, refusing to fight her.

text: All of us have the same pride in our hearts. The pride of shouldering the weight of the Gotei 13.
From the very moment that my own life was prolonged, I reflected on that this day would come. If I die, then it is my deepest wish to do so for the sake of Soul Society.
context: To Rukia Kuchiki about using Kamikake.

text: I thought you would be here, Captain Aizen. No. I should not call you 'Captain' anymore, should I? Treacherous reprobate, Sousuke Aizen.
context: To Sousuke Aizen, confronting him at the Central 46 chambers.

text: While it is good to be energetic, please remain quiet inside the hospital. As long as you are still inside this building, your lives are in our hands. Please do not forget that.
context: To two 11th Division Shinigami (Ikkaku and Yumichika) inside the 4th Division barracks.

text: The act of saving a comrade in need should require no thanks.
context: To Shinji Hirako after healing the Vizards.

text: There is only one Kenpachi every era. That is a rule, and also an unavoidable destiny. Because when a strong person finds another strong one, they stop using the sword just for themselves. Suddenly, their sword is used to either kill or raise the other strong person.
context: Reflecting on the history of the Kenpachi title.

text: I am strong. Stronger than anyone but you. That is why I must take your life this day. A hundred times, or even a thousand. That is why I must bring you healing. No matter how many times it takes. Until you are restored...to your true self. Until finally, you can surpass me, and move on to far greater heights...Come.
context: Thinking about Kenpachi Zaraki during their battle to the death.

text: Your transformation is not a disease. You were just given the key to the door in front of you. There's no need to know the cause. No need for sorrow or sadness. What comes next is up to you. You can use your keys to open the door...or lock it tight. And if you do choose to open it...you can still decide not to pass through.
context: To Yasutora Sado and Orihime Inoue regarding their developing powers.

text: Urgent, meet us outside the Urahara Shop immediately. P.S: If you're thinking that this message is like some written in the victim's own blood cliché from some TV show...then you obviously have no sense of humor!

There is nothing, but fear reflected in your sword. When you dodge, you're afraid of getting killed. When you attack, you're afraid of killing someone. Even when you try to protect someone, you're afraid of letting them die. Yes, your sword speaks to me only of absurd fear. What's necessary in a fight isn't fear. Nothing can be born of that. When you dodge, "I won't let them cut me." If you protect someone, "I won't let them die." If you attack, "I will kill them." Well, can't you see the resolve to kill you in my sword?
Puh-Lease! I'm just a lowly-but-handsome merchant. How could I possibly perform Bankai?
context: To Renji Abarai while deflecting questions about his power.

text: A warrior who has lost his strength will only get in the way.
context: To Orihime Inoue regarding the nature of battle.

text: I won't get hit by any more of those. I've analyzed that technique's Reishi composition and even the patterns in your muscle movements when you fire. If I know those two things, I can dodge it or by timing it right, I can cancel it out. But that's difficult in its own right.
context: To Yammy Llargo during their encounter.

text: I made a decision when I got out of bed this morning. 'Once I get out of this bed, I'm a member of the Twelfth Division. From now on, I'm only going to get angry if someone badmouths the Twelfth Division.' After all, I think changing what makes you mad and what doesn't is kind of basically the same thing as 'changing your own feelings'. Right?
That I was never able to fully control. True enough. Back then, I couldn't.
context: To Sousuke Aizen regarding the Hōgyoku.

text: You give me too much credit. These days, I'm just a plain old candy-store owner.
context: To Sousuke Aizen during their confrontation.

text: If we want to witness something nobody has ever seen before, we'll create it ourselves. That's how us scientists are.
If I prepare a thousand options, all I need is for one of them to work. So I make a habit of preparing anything and everything that shows any kind of promise. That's just how I work.
What’s so outrageous? This is a real battle. If you lose, you die. Taking every possible precaution to avoid death...isn't that what everyone does?
My quarry never escapes. Come, you rascals. But you won't get off without a thrashing this time...
context: To Shunsui Kyōraku and Jūshirō Ukitake, warning them before engaging Aizen.

text: Listen well! Be gone from here! I don't have time nor the patience to teach a mere infant like you how to breathe.
I was proud of you as if you were my own sons. I believed that, even if the character is different, it is fine if they took the same paths.
context: To Shunsui Kyōraku and Jūshirō Ukitake.

text: As I taught you before, there is no mercy for he who obstructs justice.
context: To Shunsui Kyōraku and Jūshirō Ukitake during their battle.

text: There is no individual sense of justice that can overrule the justice of the world.
context: To Shunsui Kyōraku and Jūshirō Ukitake.

text: Place everything you have on the line, and crush them where they stand! Even if they should tear your flesh from your bones, make every last fragment of those bones an iron wall! They must not be permitted to set a single foot upon Soul Society's ground!
context: Commanding the Gotei 13 during the Wandenreich invasion.

text: Dear me. Forcing the Captain-Commander himself to come to their aid. What are today's officers coming to?
context: To Izuru Kira after arriving on the battlefield.

text: It seems you are a simple monster who knows nothing but slaughter. A pity.
It is time for you to perish alongside me in this blazing inferno.
They give their lives to vanquish a great evil. That is the nature of the Gotei 13.
context: To Sousuke Aizen regarding his subordinates.

text: Do you believe that simply by sealing Ryūjin Jakka, you have defeated me? You fool. Your foolishness is so great, it astounds me. Why, precisely, do you think I have served as Captain-Commander of the Gotei 13 for a thousand years? Because in all those thousand years, no Shinigami was born to rival me in strength.
I am glad that you at least abandoned your childlike form. As a result, I can slaughter you without hesitation.
Now listen! Victory is not the only thing that matters on the field of battle! What exactly do you fools take your Captain's robes for, anyway?!
context: To Byakuya Kuchiki, Kenpachi Zaraki, and Shunsui Kyōraku.

text: Regardless of the circumstances, we were saved by Ichigo Kurosaki in our time of need. It is now time that we returned the favor. Even if that should require violating our own statutes, to allow such a debt to go unpaid would bring eternal shame upon the entire Gotei 13.
context: To the Gotei 13 regarding Ichigo's power restoration.

text: I am here. What better security can there be?
context: To Luders Friegen upon arriving at the scene.

text: Ah, that picture... That is a monster that appeared in Soul Society long ago. It appeared when Soul Society was in great need and only brought further turmoil. It will not trouble us again. If, someday, that monster were to appear before us again... I fear that will be the last day I am ever seen in this place.
context: To a young Shunsui Kyōraku about the Ukitake/Kyoraku history.

text: I'd forgotten it for a long time...the pain of not having a name. Those other guys had names that others called them, but I didn't. So, that kinda feelin'...
context: Reflecting on his sword while learning its name.

text: I named you Yachiru, in memory of the only person I ever admired.
context: To Yachiru Kusajishi regarding her name.

text: I want to get stronger. I've finally found a worthy opponent. I can get stronger. I want to fight. I want to get stronger. It sure has been a while. To feel this desire again. I want to get stronger.
You are shameless to sneak around like that, guys. Cowardly hiding spiritual pressure and stalking others isn't something that captains should do.
context: Calling out Sajin Komamura and Kaname Tōsen.

text: Blah blah blah! You're noisy! Just come on and fight already. It's best if all four of you come together. If you four surround me and attack at once, maybe one of you will actually wound me.
context: Challenging Komamura, Tōsen, Iba, and Hisagi.

text: Sanity? Worthless things like that, I never had them for as long as I can remember!
So you want to fight too? Fine, you can have them, but fight elsewhere. If you get in my way, I'll chop you up first.
context: To Ikkaku Madarame and Yumichika Ayasegawa.

text: This Reiatsu... Even though I don't know who it's from, they seem to be deep in the heat of battle. Not bad. It sure makes your blood boil, doesn't it?

Never admit defeat and ask for a quick death! Die first, then admit defeat! If you are defeated but didn't die, it just means you were lucky! At those times, think only about survival! Survive and think only about killing the one who failed to kill you!
context: Teaching Ikkaku Madarame his philosophy of battle.

text: Sorry but I'm not interested in weaklings who can't fight any more. Besides, I don't have any obligation to finish you off.
Not criminals? That's rich, coming from you. You know damn well that if we weren't so damn powerful, petty criminals is all the both of us would be!

What’s that face for? Did you 'imagine' I wouldn’t be able to cut you? Just so you know, there ain't a damn thing your little brain can come up with that I won't be able to slice through. I'm the Kenpachi.
context: To Gremmy Thoumeaux during their duel.

text: I hate to talk like I'm better'n you or whatever, but I'd like to just give you a little tip, as a guy who's had a little experience at this job. When ya find yourself in a position of command, even if ya sympathize with 'em, ya can't be too sensitive to your subordinates' feelings. You do things how you wanna do 'em. An' if doin' it your own way doesn't work out, then that just means the job ain't for you.
'Pretty good, huh? Doing things in reverse is my specialty.
I swear. I really don't like gettin' involved in Shinigami fights. Oh well. When you hear the sound of fighting so close by you can't just ignore it.
Can't ya hold back a little while you're fighting? If I dodge an attack and the city gets destroyed bit by bit instead, then I can't just ignore it and keep dodging.

I remember what Urahara said. 'I made no miscalculations. That was my greatest miscalculation of all.' And just like he predicted, the worst-case scenario came to pass. Man, we sure owe a lot to them. To Urahara, and also to Aizen, an' all. Let's go.
context: Reflecting on Urahara's past warnings before heading into battle.

text: Can't say I've got much to settle with you. Although I do kinda wish you guys hadn't gone an' set up that crazy strong barrier before you started fightin'!
context: To Genryūsai Shigekuni Yamamoto upon arrival.

text: Well, ain't the answer to that freakin' obvious? Not a chance. We ain't your allies or nothin'. We're just Aizen's enemies. Oh, an' we're also Ichigo's allies.
context: To Yamamoto, clarifying the Visored's position.

text: You sure 'bout that? Jumpin' in to help a bunch of total unknowns like us? Well, not that I'm quite sure what the heck you are either.
context: To Sajin Komamura regarding his interference.

text: Up, down, left, right, back, front, the directions the damage is comin' from... do you really think you can wrap your head around fightin' with them all flipped around? Not a chance. Ain't no-one can do that. The stronger a guy is, the more used to battle he gets... the more naturally his body reacts to exactly what it sees!!
context: To Sousuke Aizen, explaining the nature of his Shikai.

text: Oh, mine only lasts about three minutes. I'm a hero, y'see.
context: To Coyote Starrk, discussing his Hollow mask duration.

text: Is that right. How about you try and aesthetically not get yourself buried in the first place in the future.
context: To Rose, dismissing his complaints about his hair.

text: Once a guy's comrade falls, the battle gets personal.
context: To Rose, commenting on the nature of Starrk's battle.

text: That's what makes him so scary. There's not a guy in the world who isn't affected at all by a comrade's death. If his comrade's death causes him to falter, we take advantage. If his comrade's death drives him to anger, we strike him down before he can let it out. In either case, speed is of the essence!
context: Explaining the strategy for fighting Starrk to Rose.

text: Now that's a surprise! I always thought you Hollow scum didn't have hearts or feelings!
context: To Starrk, reacting to his emotional response regarding his comrades.

text: Bahhhhhh~~! What are you, Kensei, stupid~? How long d'you think I can stay in Hollow form for, huh?! It's fifteen hours, okay? Fifteen hours! I could stay like this right into the night if I wanted to!
context: To Kensei Muguruma, bragging about her Hollow mask duration.

text: How dare you. You ruined my precious scarf! I'm not forgiving you now!!
context: To Wonderweiss Margela after being struck.

text: K... Kensei...... Are you gonna... avenge me......? Heheh...
context: To Kensei Muguruma after being defeated by Wonderweiss.

text: You're alive! You should be happy! So smile!
Sorry, brat. I'm not mature enough to hold back just 'cause you're a kid!
I bet he's happy, because we hardly meet anybody with powers similar to ours. I feel like our family is growing.
context: About Hachigen Ushōda and Orihime Inoue.

text: Come along. That's right... right this way. Slowly does it... closer and closer... until you are captive to my melody.

Banding together to face a common enemy is sometimes referred to as a dark trait of humanity, but that's not quite right. It is no 'dark trait', but rather a basic animal survival instinct. Frankly, it is at times such as these that human emotional connection is at its most-
